item.prompt:
You are coordinating SocialPredict task PR581-010 from /workspace/spec-socialpredict-tasks against target repo /workspace/socialpredict/backend. Follow AGENTS.md from the task repo and target repo guidance. You own coordination: decide whether to spawn specialist agents, wait for them, and return a concrete execution plan plus any findings needed for implementation.

Task scope is strict and comes only from this metadata:
- Only unchecked math files / exact symbols listed in the prompt are in scope.
- Do not broaden into checked math files unless a listed unchecked symbol requires a narrow supporting change.
- Preserve math in domain packages; do not push math into handlers.
- Canonical report dir: /workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/PR581-010
- Specialists append facts/decisions; dispatcher owns summary direction but top-level agent will finalize summary.json.
- Prefer incremental report reads.
- If summary.json.context.handoff_requested becomes true, stop branching, record handoff, and converge.

Required deliverable from you:
1. Decide the smallest useful specialist set, if any.
2. Wait for specialists before concluding.
3. Return a concise plan: touched files/symbols, recommended implementation order, validation plan, and any risks/assumptions.
4. If you use specialists, summarize each result clearly enough that I can implement without redoing their work.

item.aggregated_output:
---
name: socialpredict-go-testing-reliability
description: Run deterministic Go reliability checks for SocialPredict backend/API changes. Use when implementing or reviewing backend domain/service/repository/handler changes, diagnosing flaky test behavior, or preparing verification evidence.
---

# SocialPredict Go Testing Reliability

## Workflow

1. Keep execution limited to backend/API changes on PR #581 lineage.
2. Read `references/testing-reliability-guide.md` for deterministic test expectations.
3. Run `scripts/run_reliability_suite.sh [repo-dir] [pkg-pattern]`.
4. Repeat tests until results are deterministic or escalate as flaky-risk.
5. Log command outputs and failure triage notes in the task note.

## Current PR Execution Scope

- Prioritize service-level and integration-relevant backend tests for touched packages.
- Keep verification focused on changed backend/API behavior.
- Do not introduce unrelated test framework refactors.

## Future-State Kubernetes Design Context (Guidance Only)

- Preserve tests that remain valid when domains are extracted into separate services.
- Favor contract and integration checks that verify service boundaries, not in-process assumptions.
- Keep failure diagnostics actionable for later distributed tracing and observability workflows.

## Resources

- `references/testing-reliability-guide.md`: deterministic test policy and triage checklist.
- `scripts/run_reliability_suite.sh`: repeatable test, OpenAPI validation, and vet checks.

---
name: socialpredict-go-architecture-governance
description: Enforce SocialPredict Go service-boundary governance for backend/API work. Use when implementing or reviewing changes in backend handlers, domain services, repositories, auth service, or route wiring to prevent layer violations, direct DB leakage, and cross-domain coupling.
---

# SocialPredict Go Architecture Governance

## Workflow

1. Confirm scope is limited to `socialpredict/backend/**` and API contract artifacts.
2. Read `references/boundary-governance.md` before editing boundaries or wiring.
3. Run `scripts/run_boundary_checks.sh [repo-dir]`.
4. Treat any new boundary violation as a blocker and stop implementation until fixed.
5. Report command output and violating file paths in the task note or review.

## Current PR Execution Scope

- Keep execution on PR #581 lineage and backend/API-only paths.
- Block new direct DB access in handlers/auth outside the approved exception list.
- Avoid frontend work and infrastructure rollout in this execution wave.

## Future-State Kubernetes Design Context (Guidance Only)

- Keep domains extractable into independently deployable services.
- Keep concrete wiring centralized so service extraction remains straightforward.
- Preserve interface-first boundaries so health/readiness and observability evolve cleanly later.

## Resources

- `references/boundary-governance.md`: rules, exceptions, escalation expectations.
- `scripts/run_boundary_checks.sh`: deterministic boundary and import checks.

---
name: socialpredict-go-code-quality-guardrails
description: Apply Go quality guardrails for SocialPredict backend/API work. Use when touching backend Go files to enforce formatting, vet hygiene, and backend boundary-safe coding practices before review or verifier handoff.
---

# SocialPredict Go Code Quality Guardrails

## Workflow

1. Confirm changed paths stay in backend/API scope.
2. Read `references/code-quality-checklist.md` for required quality gates.
3. Run `scripts/run_quality_guardrails.sh [repo-dir] [base-ref]`.
4. Resolve all blocking findings before handoff.
5. Include command output and touched-file list in the verification notes.

## Current PR Execution Scope

- Apply quality checks to backend/API files changed in the PR #581 wave.
- Enforce `gofmt` and `go vet` as non-optional gates.
- Avoid broad stylistic refactors unrelated to the current change set.

## Future-State Kubernetes Design Context (Guidance Only)

- Preserve configuration hygiene and observability-friendly error handling for eventual service extraction.
- Keep code changes diagnosable under distributed service operation.
- Maintain quality standards that support future independent service rollout.

## Resources

- `references/code-quality-checklist.md`: required gates and escalation criteria.
- `scripts/run_quality_guardrails.sh`: changed-file gofmt, go vet, and boundary hygiene checks.
- Companion tool skills for deeper or tool-specific passes:
  - `socialpredict-go-gofmt`
  - `socialpredict-go-vet`
  - `socialpredict-go-staticcheck`
  - `socialpredict-go-golangci-lint`
  - `socialpredict-go-cyclomatic-complexity`

282:def command_read_summary(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
290:def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
291:    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
292:    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
294:    init_parser = subparsers.add_parser("init")
295:    init_parser.add_argument("--report-dir", required=True)
296:    init_parser.add_argument("--task-id", required=True)
297:    init_parser.add_argument("--title", required=True)
298:    init_parser.add_argument("--working-dir", required=True)
299:    init_parser.add_argument("--dispatcher-agent", required=True)
300:    init_parser.add_argument("--control-repo", required=True)
301:    init_parser.add_argument("--target-repo", required=True)
302:    init_parser.set_defaults(func=command_init)
304:    event_parser = subparsers.add_parser("append-event")
305:    event_parser.add_argument("--report-dir", required=True)
306:    event_parser.add_argument("--agent-name", required=True)
307:    event_parser.add_argument("--agent-role", required=True)
308:    event_parser.add_argument("--event-type", required=True)
309:    event_parser.add_argument("--summary", required=True)
310:    event_parser.add_argument("--status")
311:    event_parser.add_argument("--files-considered", action="append")
312:    event_parser.add_argument("--files-changed", action="append")
313:    event_parser.add_argument("--checks-run", action="append")
314:    event_parser.add_argument("--refs", action="append")
315:    event_parser.set_defaults(func=command_append_event)
317:    decision_parser = subparsers.add_parser("append-decision")
318:    decision_parser.add_argument("--report-dir", required=True)
319:    decision_parser.add_argument("--agent-name", required=True)
320:    decision_parser.add_argument("--decision", required=True)
321:    decision_parser.add_argument("--reason", required=True)
322:    decision_parser.add_argument("--impact")
323:    decision_parser.set_defaults(func=command_append_decision)
325:    summary_parser = subparsers.add_parser("update-summary")
326:    summary_parser.add_argument("--report-dir", required=True)
327:    summary_parser.add_argument("--task-id")
328:    summary_parser.add_argument("--owner")
329:    summary_parser.add_argument("--status")
330:    summary_parser.add_argument("--headline")
331:    summary_parser.add_argument("--current-focus")
332:    summary_parser.add_argument("--last-event-seq", type=int)
333:    summary_parser.add_argument("--files-changed", action="append")
334:    summary_parser.add_argument("--checks-run", action="append")
335:    summary_parser.add_argument("--open-questions", action="append")
336:    summary_parser.add_argument("--follow-ups", action="append")
337:    summary_parser.set_defaults(func=command_update_summary)
339:    context_parser = subparsers.add_parser("update-context")
340:    context_parser.add_argument("--report-dir", required=True)
341:    context_parser.add_argument("--session-id")
342:    context_parser.add_argument("--used-tokens", required=True, type=int)
343:    context_parser.add_argument("--context-window", required=True, type=int)
344:    context_parser.add_argument("--remaining-tokens", required=True, type=int)
345:    context_parser.add_argument("--used-pct", required=True, type=float)
346:    context_parser.add_argument("--soft-threshold-pct", required=True, type=float)
347:    context_parser.add_argument("--threshold-pct", required=True, type=float)
348:    context_parser.add_argument("--handoff-requested", action="store_true")
349:    context_parser.add_argument("--reason")
350:    context_parser.set_defaults(func=command_update_context)
352:    read_events = subparsers.add_parser("read-events")
358:    read_decisions = subparsers.add_parser("read-decisions")
364:    read_summary = subparsers.add_parser("read-summary")
368:    return parser
372:    parser = build_parser()
373:    args = parser.parse_args()

items.text: Refactor scoped production math helpers in positions for narrower responsibilities without changing behavior
Refactor scoped market/dbpm/positions tests into smaller reusable helpers while preserving existing expectations
Run focused formatting and verification, update canonical task report, and summarize results

019d58b7-44ee-7c60-95f0-5c0da73b8dfa.message:
Dispatcher review complete for `PR581-010`.

Smallest useful specialist set:
- `architecture-agent`: needed for SRP boundaries in `positions/adjust_valuation.go` and `positions/profitability.go`.
- `test-reliability-agent`: needed to keep the refactor limited to helper extraction and stable deterministic tests.
- No broader specialists are needed; this should not touch handlers, OpenAPI, migrations, or checked math implementation files.

Specialist conclusions to apply:
- Architecture guidance:
  - Keep all changes inside the listed symbols/files.
  - In `adjust_valuation.go`, separate responsibilities into: selection/filtering of eligible valuations, deterministic ranking policy, and delta application. `AdjustUserValuationsToMarketVolume` should remain a thin orchestrator.
  - In `profitability.go`, separate user-level data derivation from leaderboard assembly/sorting. `CalculateMarketLeaderboard` should orchestrate calls to small helpers; `CalculateUserSpend`, `GetEarliestBetTime`, and `DeterminePositionType` stay domain math utilities.
  - Do not move any math upward into handlers or sideways into unrelated packages.
- Test reliability guidance:
  - Replace repeated inline fixture setup with small local helpers in the scoped test files only.
  - Prefer fixed timestamps over `time.Now()` where deterministic ordering matters.
  - In `dbpm/marketshares_test.go`, extract shared test data builders and assertion helpers instead of repeating payout/bet setup blocks.
  - Keep assertions behaviorally equivalent; avoid broad golden-data rewrites.

Recommended implementation order:
1. `internal/domain/math/positions/adjust_valuation.go`
   - Extract ranking/adjustment helpers so `AdjustUserValuationsToMarketVolume` only coordinates.
   - Keep `UserHolder`/`ByValBetTimeUsername` focused on ordering semantics only.
2. `internal/domain/math/positions/profitability.go`
   - Add small helpers for building one `UserProfitability` row and for ranking/sorting the result slice.
   - Keep the existing exported helper functions intact unless a very small signature-safe extraction improves SRP.
3. Scoped tests:
   - `positions/adjust_valuation_test.go`
   - `positions/profitability_test.go`
   - `positions/valuation_test.go`
   - `market/dust_test.go`
   - `market/marketvolume_test.go`
   - `outcomes/dbpm/marketshares_test.go`
   - Extract local fixtures/builders/assertions per file, especially for repeated bets, payouts, and timestamps.

Touched files/symbols to expect:
- `internal/domain/math/positions/adjust_valuation.go`
  - `UserHolder`
  - `ByValBetTimeUsername`
  - `Len`, `Swap`, `Less`
  - `AdjustUserValuationsToMarketVolume`
  - `filterWinningValuations`
  - `buildUserHolders`
  - `adjustValuations`
- `internal/domain/math/positions/profitability.go`
  - `UserProfitability`
  - `CalculateMarketLeaderboard`
  - `CalculateUserSpend`
  - `GetEarliestBetTime`
  - `DeterminePositionType`
- The listed scoped test symbols in the prompt only.

Validation plan:
- Run targeted package tests only:
  - `go test ./internal/domain/math/market ./internal/domain/math/outcomes/dbpm ./internal/domain/math/positions`
- If the refactor changes only helper structure and tests, that should be sufficient for this task.
- Optionally run `gofmt -w` on touched Go files before tests.

Risks / assumptions:
- `dust.go`, `marketvolume.go`, and other checked math implementation files should remain unchanged unless one of the listed unchecked tests needs a very narrow compatibility update.
- There are unrelated existing worktree changes elsewhere in `/workspace/socialpredict`; avoid those files entirely.
- `marketshares_test.go` is the largest SRP surface, but it appears to need test-helper extraction only, not production-code changes.

task_id: `PR581-010`  
status: `completed`

Refactored the scoped SRP targets in [adjust_valuation.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/math/positions/adjust_valuation.go), [profitability.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/math/positions/profitability.go), [dust_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/math/market/dust_test.go), [marketvolume_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/math/market/marketvolume_test.go), [marketshares_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/math/outcomes/dbpm/marketshares_test.go), [adjust_valuation_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/math/positions/adjust_valuation_test.go), [profitability_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/math/positions/profitability_test.go), and [valuation_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/math/positions/valuation_test.go). The production changes keep math in the domain layer and split orchestration from ranking/building logic; the test changes extract local helpers, shared assertions, and deterministic timestamps without changing expected behavior.

follow_ups: `none`
